subject: Re: FW: email for Jeffrey
body:
Alex.. when you first brough this up last october,, I
told you ,, that if Igor,, earned money for you,, show
me how much,, and I would compensate you,, I then
hired you again in Novemer , and december,, past our
contract period , we differ on whether goals were
reached, you told me again that you needed money,, I
had already given more than twenty thousand dollars,
than we agreed,,, I then gave you an additional ten.
with the promise that that was the end.. you
agreed,,,even though you promised, you never showed
bella even 10,000 of igors payments to you... He says
it was never even that much... so I don't understand
how you can once again suggest you are owed money
after receiving more than we agreed ,, than an
additional ten thousan d,, and never once showing us
what you promised to last summer..  This is wrong,, I
like you and respect you,, I have paid , for more than
my training ,  trips to paris and chicago,, it is
unfair...

--- Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com> wrote:

>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
> Lesley Groff
> Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
>  
>  
> 
> ________________________________
> 
> From: Flavia Kupferberg
> [mailto:flavia@completebody.com] 
> Sent: Wednesday, September 06, 2006 12:14 PM
> To: Lesley Groff
> Subject: email for Jeffrey
> 
> 
> 
> Dear Jeffrey,
> 
>  
> 
> You previously made a number of arguments regarding
> the fact that I
> neither have neither office space nor a staff to run
> a headhunter's
> office.  I have office space and a staff to manage
> my company.  You took
> my most valuable trainer and promised to compensate
> me for the
> significant income I lost as a result.  I did not
> believe compensation
> would be a problem and calculated this money into my
> August budget which
> is my slowest month.  Last time I spoke to Bella you
> said you would
> think about it.  All I ask is that you honor our
> gentleman's agreement.
> 
>  
> 
> Kind regards,
> 
> Alex Reznik
> 
>

--- Lesley Groff lesley@nysgllc.com wrote:

Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein

From: Flavia Kupferberg
[mailto:flavia@completebody.com]
Sent: Wednesday, September 06, 2006 12:14 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: email for Jeffrey

Dear Jeffrey,

You previously made a number of arguments regarding
the fact that I
neither have neither office space nor a staff to run
a headhunter's
office. I have office space and a staff to manage
my company. You took
my most valuable trainer and promised to compensate
me for the
significant income I lost as a result. I did not
believe compensation
would be a problem and calculated this money into my
August budget which
is my slowest month. Last time I spoke to Bella you
said you would
think about it. All I ask is that you honor our
gentleman's agreement.

Kind regards,

Alex Reznik
